rulings.Case Title: ANGELEA ROMBEIRO VS. JESSICA WRIGHT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 21, 2025 line 1. ATTORNEY'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL (RE: PLAINTIFF ANGELEA ROMBEIRO) is OFF CALENDAR. No Proof of Service on file showing Plaintiff was personally served. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom [Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252]. Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: DARUKA WANIGATUNGA VS. THOMAS "TOBY" A. ROEBUCK ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 21, 2025 line 2. ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION is CONTINUED to May 1, 2025 for Defendant to comply with SFLR 2.7B and provide courtesy copies of the opposition no later than April 22, 2025 with a cover letter reflecting new hearing date. The Court further notes that Plaintiff failed to file a proof of service by March 28, 2025 as ordered. Proof of service must be on file no later than April 22, 2025. =(501) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom [Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252]. Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: CAROLYN CAIN VS. MICHELLE JESTER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 21, 2025 line 4. DEFENDANT MICHELLE JESTER'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part, with leave to amend within 5 days of notice of entry of order, and OVERRULED in part. Based on the allegation that defendant shares non-exclusive possession of the premises with another occupant who has permission to remain, the second amended complaint for unlawful detainer seeks an impermissible partial eviction which would not restore possession of the premises to plaintiff, and the demurrer is sustained on this ground. The demurrer is otherwise overruled. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom [Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252]. Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MISSION BAY BLOCK 7 HOUSING PARTNERS, LP VS. ARMANI BUTLER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 21, 2025 line 5. DEFENDANT ARMANI BUTLER'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part, with leave to amend within 5 days of notice of entry of order, and OVERRULED in part. The allegations in the Complaint and the attached proof of service are inconsistent regarding the date of alleged service, rendering the Complaint uncertain on its face, and the demurrer is sustained on this ground. The demurrer is otherwise overruled. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom [Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252]. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
